summary: Recipe drives the live extension end-to-end on home.html. AC1 (mount persists), AC4 (in-app leave clears), and AC3 (home consumes seeded entry and redirects) are PROVEN with strict assertions + screenshots. AC2 (popup teardown survival), AC5 (TTL > 5min), AC6 (popup vs fullscreen parity), and AC7 (pendingRedirectRoute precedence) are explicitly UNTESTABLE on this CDP target and are covered by the home.component.test.tsx and app-state-controller.test.ts unit suites.

checks.name: ac_to_node_binding
checks.reason: Every recipe node exercising an AC carries an ac<N>- prefix; setup/teardown nodes use the proper plumbing prefixes; trace.json node IDs match the drafted recipe one-for-one (21 executed nodes including done).

checks.name: assertion_rigor
checks.reason: ac1-assert-state, ac4-assert-cleared, and ac3-assert-redirect each pin an exact hash + state shape (eq/falsy/gt). Reverting the PR change would flip these asserts: removing setLastVisitedPerpsRoute on PerpsLayout mount would fail ac1, removing the cleanup-phase clear+marker would fail ac4, removing checkLastVisitedPerpsRoute in home would fail ac3.

checks.name: visual_evidence
checks.reason: Three screenshots (BTC market detail, wallet home, ETH market detail) match their AC captions verbatim. Caption-to-image self-review found no mismatches.

checks.name: untestable_documentation
checks.reason: AC2/5/6/7 are pre-flagged in TASK step 10b/10c with concrete rationale (CDP target attach scope, no back-dating API, deterministic-ordering limitation) and routed to specific named unit tests.

checks.name: forbidden_patterns
checks.reason: No switch+default bypass, no eval_sync returning a skip reason, no wait>500ms used as wait_for substitute (the only wait is the 2000ms in-app marker decay, which is a real time-decay tied to wasPerpsUnmountedInAppRecently(1500)), no DOM-only ordering claims, all node IDs prefixed correctly.

checks.name: trace_self_consistency
checks.reason: trace.json reports 20/20 executed nodes ok=true plus done. No drafted node missing or skipped.

untestable_acs.reason: popup teardown survival — only home.html target is reachable on this CDP attach
untestable_acs.covered_by: home.component.test.tsx in-app-marker race test + useLayoutEffect cleanup ordering

untestable_acs.reason: no API to back-date the timestamp; live 6-minute wait impractical
untestable_acs.covered_by: home.component.test.tsx 'does not redirect but still clears when TTL has expired'

untestable_acs.reason: popup.html target not reachable via CDP without a user gesture
untestable_acs.covered_by: implementation is single-bundle; AC1/3/4 home.html proof transfers

untestable_acs.reason: deterministic ordering of pendingRedirectRoute + lastVisitedPerpsRoute in a single componentDidUpdate cycle is not reliably reproducible from a CDP recipe
untestable_acs.covered_by: home.component.test.tsx 'defers to pendingRedirectRoute when both are set but still clears the persisted perps entry'

better_version_guidance: Add a bundled domains/extension-core/flows/seed-app-state-route flow that wraps `submitRequestToBackground('setLastVisitedPerpsRoute', ...)` + `setPendingRedirectRoute` so future precedence-style recipes can reuse this seed pattern instead of inlining eval_async.

Expose a debug-only `setLastVisitedPerpsRouteWithTimestamp(path, timestamp)` background method gated on a build flag so AC5 (TTL expiry) can be exercised by a recipe that seeds a stale timestamp directly. Without it, AC5 will remain UNTESTABLE in live CDP.

If popup-target attach lands in farmslot, add an AC2/AC6 split run that closes and reopens the popup target between the persist and the consume nodes — the only piece of behaviour not currently exercised end-to-end on home.html.

The 1500ms in-app-marker grace is a magic number duplicated between PerpsLayout cleanup (writes) and home.component (reads). Pull it into `ui/helpers/constants/routes.ts` or alongside `PERPS_REOPEN_TTL_MS` as `PERPS_IN_APP_LEAVE_GRACE_MS` so it stays in sync if either side changes.
